summary: High‑energy Deer Creek show with soaring Jerry, beastly Phil, and stellar jams
blurb: The set opens with a smoking Jack Straw and delivers tight performances of Desolation Row, the Playing > China Doll > Uncle John’s sandwich, and a powerful Watchtower‑Black Peter‑Not Fade Away climax. Jerry’s lead guitar soars while Phil’s bass drives the band, and Brent’s keyboards are in top form. The show stays strong throughout both sets.
review:
On July 19, 1990 at Deer Creek Music Center the Grateful Dead served up a summer‑long blast of energy that still feels fresh. Kicking off with an electrifying Jack Straw, the first set rolled through They Love Each Other, Desolation Row and a soulful Althea before closing with The Promised Land. The second set was a tour de force: Victim Or The Crime segued into Foolish Heart, then exploded into a seamless Playing In The Band → China Doll → Uncle John's Band sequence that kept the dance floor moving. After a dynamic drum and space segment, the band launched into an inspired All Along The Watchtower that melted into Black Peter and a jubilant Not Fade Away, before wrapping with a rousing U.S. Blues encore.

Across all available recordings the performance is consistently praised: Jerry’s lead work is described as “soaring” and “on fire,” delivering scorching solos on Jack Straw, Althea, Row Jimmy and beyond. Phil’s bass is “beastly,” “in charge,” and at times “so loud it drives the groove.” Brent Mydland shines with tight keyboards and memorable solo moments, while Mickey Hart (and Bill Kreutzmann) provide a powerful rhythmic foundation peppered with spacey, funk‑laden textures. The best listening experience comes from the Miller soundboard capture (gd1990-07-19.sbd.miller.32354.sbeok.flac16), which delivers crystal‑clear fidelity and captures every nuance of this high‑octane performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All eight recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly praising Jerry’s soaring solos, Phil’s beastly bass, Brent’s top‑form keyboards and the overall high energy of the show. Only one audience tape notes a weaker second set, but the consensus across soundboard and matrix sources is that the performance is exceptional.

best_recording.reason: Excellent Miller soundboard capture—crisp, clear mix with no drop‑outs, preserving the full dynamics of Jerry’s lead guitar, Phil’s booming bass and Brent’s tight keyboards.

key_highlights: Smoking Jack Straw opener sets a high‑energy tone
Epic Playing > China Doll > Uncle John’s sandwich drives the second set
Watchtower → Black Peter → Not Fade Away sequence delivers a powerful climax
Brent Mydland’s keyboards and Mickey Hart’s spacey drumming elevate the jam sections

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring lead guitar throughout; scorching solos on Jack Straw, Althea, Row Jimmy and a fire‑brand presence that lifts the entire band.
band_performance.Phil: Beastly, booming bass that’s both audible and driving; repeatedly described as ‘in charge’ and essential to the show’s momentum.
band_performance.Bob: Fired‑up rhythm guitar and strong vocal contributions, adding grit especially on early set numbers.
band_performance.Brent: Top‑form keyboards with tight solo work (notably on Row Jimmy) that defines the late‑era sound; his playing is a highlight of the performance.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides crazy sound effects, spacey and funkadelic passages, and a powerful rhythmic foundation throughout the jams.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers solid dual‑drum support alongside Mickey, keeping the groove tight and energetic.
